About.vision: We shift the core design parameter of AI education systems from 'amount of help' to 'quality of friction.'
About.philosophy: Just as Socrates — the 'Gadfly' of Athens — relentlessly posed annoying questions, we research how AI can provide precisely calibrated cognitive friction to strengthen critical thinking.
About.description: Gadfly AI Lab proposes a new paradigm for educational AI. Starting from the insight that 'frictionless' AI tutoring systems paradoxically accelerate metacognitive laziness, we design and validate an Educational Friction Framework inspired by the physics of friction.
About.quote: On a frictionless ice surface, no matter how hard you push, you cannot move forward.

r1.title: Educational Friction Framework
r1.description: Designing and validating the Educational Friction Framework F_edu = μ_AI × N_learner, inspired by the physics of friction (f=μN).

r2.title: Adversarial AI for Collaborative Learning
r2.description: The Annoying Socrates — an adversarial AI teammate model that promotes critical thinking through deliberate cognitive friction in CSCL.

r3.title: Cultural AI Design
r3.description: AI Red Team mechanism — friction calibration adapted to cultural contexts (face-saving cultures) and social shielding design.

r4.title: Student Agency & Learning Acceleration
r4.description: Strengthening student agency through Appeal systems, causal thinking training, and Progressive Overload-based growth spirals.

r5.title: Real-time Adaptive Education
r5.description: Achieving agile education through LLM-based real-time N_learner estimation and dynamic μ_AI adjustment.

gskim.name: GS. Kim
gskim.fields: Education Systems · AI · Library & Information Science

p1.title: The Annoying Socrates: A Friction-Based Adversarial AI Model for Collaborative Learning
p1.authors: Ko, H., Kim, G., & Kim, J.
p1.keywords: Adversarial AI · Cognitive Friction · Collaborative Learning · Socratic Method
